---
title: "The Clo-Author"
subtitle: "AI Research Architecture for Medical and Clinical Research"
---

An open-source workflow that turns your terminal into a clinical-research assistant — from literature review to protocol design, analysis, manuscript drafting, peer review, and submission.

This fork is calibrated by default to **pulmonary medicine / interventional pulmonology**, but the structure generalizes across medical specialties.

## Get Started in 5 Minutes

### 1. Fork and clone

```bash
gh repo fork hugosantanna/clo-author --clone
cd clo-author
```

### 2. Start Claude Code

```bash
claude
```

### 3. Paste a starter prompt

> I am starting a new clinical research project in **[YOUR FIELD]** on **[YOUR TOPIC]**.
> Read `CLAUDE.md` and help me set up the project structure.
> Start with `/discover interview [YOUR TOPIC]`.

### 4. Start working

- `/discover lit [topic]` — evidence review via PubMed / Cochrane / registries
- `/strategize [question]` — study design or protocol planning
- `/analyze [dataset]` — tables, figures, and analysis scripts
- `/review --peer [journal]` — simulated journal review

## Included Validation Fixtures

This repo keeps a few minimal source files on purpose so you can verify the toolchain before starting a real project:

- `paper/main.tex` with `paper/references.bib`
- `paper/talks/full_talk.tex`
- `paper/quarto/full_talk.qmd`

They are smoke-test fixtures for manuscript, Beamer, and Quarto compilation. Replace them later if you want, but they are intentionally valid starter references.

## What Happens Behind the Scenes

Claude orchestrates specialized agents in worker-critic pairs:

- librarian + librarian-critic
- explorer + explorer-critic
- strategist + strategist-critic
- coder / data-engineer + coder-critic
- writer + writer-critic
- editor + domain-referee + methods-referee
- storyteller + storyteller-critic

The workflow is medical-first:

- reporting-guideline aware
- ethics / registration aware
- clinical-significance aware
- journal-calibrated for medical and respiratory venues

## Day 1 Checklist

- [ ] Fork and clone the repo
- [ ] Run `claude` in the project directory
- [ ] Review `CLAUDE.md`
- [ ] Customize the domain profile
- [ ] Run `/discover interview [topic]`
- [ ] Approve the first plan and review the outputs

## Where to Go Next

- [User Guide](user-guide.qmd)
- [Meet the Agents](agents.qmd)
- [Customization](customization.qmd)
- [Command Reference](reference.qmd)
